fsx.storagevirtualmachine.CfnAbout: Creates a storage virtual machine (SVM) for an Amazon FSx for ONTAP file system.

fsx.storagevirtualmachine.ActiveDirector.Description: Describes the Microsoft Active Directory configuration to which the SVM is joined, if applicable.

fsx.storagevirtualmachine.FileSystemId.Description: Specifies the FSx for ONTAP file system on which to create the SVM.

fsx.storagevirtualmachine.RootVolumeSecu.Description: The security style of the root volume of the SVM. Specify one of the following values:  +  `UNIX` if the file system is managed by a UNIX administrator, the majority of users are NFS clients, and an application accessing the data uses a UNIX user as the service account.+  `NTFS` if the file system is managed by a Windows administrator, the majority of users are SMB clients, and an application accessing the data uses a Windows user as the service account.+  `MIXED` if the file system is managed by both UNIX and Windows administrators and users consist of both NFS and SMB clients.

fsx.storagevirtualmachine.SvmAdminPasswo.Description: Specifies the password to use when logging on to the SVM using a secure shell (SSH) connection to the SVM's management endpoint. Doing so enables you to manage the SVM using the NetApp ONTAP CLI or REST API. If you do not specify a password, you can still use the file system's `fsxadmin` user to manage the SVM. For more information, see [ Managing SVMs using the NetApp ONTAP CLI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/fsx/latest/ONTAPGuide/managing-resources-ontap-apps.html#vsadmin-ontap-cli) in the *FSx for ONTAP User Guide*.

fsx.storagevirtualmachine.Tags.Description: An array of key-value pairs to apply to this resource.  For more information, see [Tag](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-resource-tags.html).
